segments.paradiso-7-g1: A liturgical vocative/interjection, an acclamation used to address God or the assembly; set off by a comma it functions as the opening cry of the hymn.
segments.paradiso-7-g2: "sanctus" is an adjective placed before the noun, agreeing masculine singular with "Deus"; the phrase is a vocative address to God. "sabaòth" is a Hebraic epithet in apposition (postpositive), effectively 'of hosts' or 'Sabaoth' following the divine name.
segments.paradiso-7-g3: A participial phrase modifying the addressed God: "superillustrans" is a present active participle meaning 'illuminating' or 'making very bright'; "claritate tua" is an instrumental phrase ('by/with your clarity'); "felices ignes horum malacòth" is the direct-object phrase ('the blessed fires of these malacoth'), with word order inverted for poetic effect.
segments.paradiso-7-g4: Sentence-final punctuation: a full stop ending the exclamation/hymn line.
segments.paradiso-7-g5: An adverb meaning 'thus' or 'so', introducing the result or manner of what follows; links this action to what preceded.
segments.paradiso-7-g6: "volgendosi" is a reflexive present participle (literally 'turning itself/turning toward'), here functioning as a circumstantial participial clause meaning 'turning toward'; "a la nota sua" ('to/at its note') is the prepositional phrase indicating the direction/target of the turning, with the possessive after the noun in poetic word order.
segments.paradiso-7-g7: A periphrastic, somewhat Latin-influenced passive construction: "fu" (past of 'essere') + predicate "viso" yields 'was seen'; "a me" marks the perceiver ('by me' / 'to me'); the infinitive "cantare" is the complement ('to sing'), and "essa sustanza" is the logical subject performing the singing ('that substance').
segments.paradiso-7-g8: "Sopra la qual" ('above which') begins a relative clause referring back to the previously mentioned substance; "doppio lume" is the subject ('a double light'), and "s'addua" is an archaic reflexive/intransitive verb form meaning 'gathers/comes together' (the clitic "s'" marks the reflexive/intransitive construction).
segments.paradiso-7-g9: "Ed" is the euphonically altered 'e' ('and'); the coordinated subject "essa e l’altre" ('it and the others') takes the verb "mossero" (passato remoto 'moved' / 'set in motion'), and "a sua danza" indicates purpose or direction ('to its/their dance')—they begin to dance.
segments.paradiso-7-g10: "E quasi velocissime faville" is a simile: 'and almost like very swift sparks' ("quasi" = 'as if/like'); in "mi si velar" the clitics mark a reflexive/impersonal construction where the sparks are the logical subject that 'veils' or 'covers' the speaker—"mi" (me) is the affected person and "si" is the reflexive clitic attached to the infinitive "velar"; "di sùbita distanza" ('with sudden distance') indicates the manner/quality of that veiling (a sudden removal or remoteness).
segments.paradiso-7-g11: Io dubitava is an imperfect indicative (first-person singular) expressing an ongoing mental state: 'I was hesitating / I doubted.' e dicea uses the imperfect of dire (dicea) to report continuous or repeated speech: 'and I was saying.' The sequence prepares the quoted imperative Dille as what he repeatedly urged.
segments.paradiso-7-g12: dille! is an imperative contraction (di + le) meaning 'tell her' or 'say it to her'; here it appears twice as an urgent repeated command.
segments.paradiso-7-g65: La + adjective + noun is the normal noun phrase 'the divine goodness.' La is the feminine definite article; divina agrees in gender with bontà. Bontà is the head noun meaning 'goodness.'
segments.paradiso-7-g66: che is a relative pronoun 'which' referring back to bontà; da sé = 'from itself' (da + reflexive sé) indicates origin; sperne is an archaic/poetic 3rd-person singular present of spernere meaning 'to scorn/reject' (here used transitively), and ogne livore = 'every spite/envious stain' is its object. Word order is relative clause: relative + origin phrase + verb + object.
segments.paradiso-7-g67: ardendo is the present gerund of ardere ('burning'), functioning adverbially to describe the manner ('burning in itself'); in sé repeats the reflexive idea 'in itself.' The gerund links the previous relative clause to the main verb that follows.
segments.paradiso-7-g68: sfavilla is a 3rd-person singular present 'it flashes/gleams'; sì che introduces a result/so‑that clause ('so that'); dispiega is an indicative present 'unfolds/displays'; le bellezze etterne is the direct object 'the eternal beauties.' The sequence expresses result: it flashes so that it unfolds the eternal beauties.
segments.paradiso-7-g69: Ciò che = 'that which' (demonstrative + relative clause). da lei = 'from her'; sanza mezzo = archaic 'without a middle/mediator' (sanza = senza); distilla is present indicative 'distills/flows down' (verb), and non ha poi fine = 'has no end afterwards' (negated present of avere plus adverb poi and noun fine). The clause as a whole gives the assertion about what issues from her.
segments.paradiso-7-g70: perché = 'because'; non si move is a reflexive/ergative use ('does not move itself' = 'does not shift'); la sua imprenta = 'her imprint/impression' (sua agreeing with the feminine subject); quand' ella sigilla = 'when she seals' (quand' is an abbreviated quando). The sentence gives the causal explanation: the imprint does not change when she seals, so the thing has no end.
segments.paradiso-7-g71: Ciò che introduces a relative clause 'that which / whatever'; da essa = 'from it' (essa refers to the prior feminine noun in the poem); sanza mezzo = 'without a means/middle' (sanza = without; mezzo = means/medium); piove is used metaphorically 'falls' or 'flows down'; libero è tutto is an inverted order meaning 'is entirely free' (tutto intensifies libero).
segments.paradiso-7-g72: Perché introduces the reason clause 'because'; non is negation; soggiace (soggiacere) + a governs a dative/prepositional complement meaning 'is subject to' or 'lies under the authority/power of'; la virtute is 'the virtue/power' (virtute as efficacy), and de le cose nove = 'of the new things' (archaic prepositional contraction where modern Italian would be 'delle cose nuove').
segments.paradiso-7-g73: Più is the comparative adverb 'more'; l’ is a clitic (here referring back to the feminine entity previously discussed) placed before the verb; è conforme = 'is conforming' or 'is like/consistent with'. The clause means 'the more it conforms (to it)'.
segments.paradiso-7-g74: E però links and gives the consequence 'and therefore'; più le piace is again comparative 'it pleases her/it more' using the dative clitic le (indirect object) before the verb piacere ('to please').
segments.paradiso-7-g75: Ché ('for'/'because') introduces the explanatory clause. L’ardor santo = 'the holy ardor' (ardor = passionate/energetic heat or zeal); ch’ (che) is a relative pronoun 'that/which'; ogne cosa = 'every thing'; raggia here describes the emanation or radiance ('sends rays' / 'radiates').
segments.paradiso-7-g76: Ne is used locatively/partitively 'in' or 'within' (here 'in the most resembling [thing]'); la più somigliante = 'the most resembling one' (definite article + superlative adjective); è più vivace = 'is more lively/active' (comparative).
segments.paradiso-7-g52: Ma = conjunction 'but'. io is the explicit subject 'I'. veggi’ is an archaic/poetic form of 'veggio/veggo' (present indicative 'I see' or 'I perceive'); the final apostrophe marks an elision of the last vowel. or = 'now'. la tua mente ristretta is a noun phrase 'your mind narrowed' (ristretta = past participle used adjectivally). di pensiero in pensier is a paired prepositional phrase 'from thought into thought' (shows successive mental movements), and dentro ad un nodo locates it 'within a knot' (ad is the allative form of a used before a vowel).
segments.paradiso-7-g53: del qual = 'of which' (relative phrase referring back to 'nodo'). con gran disio = 'with great desire' (gran = shortened grande). solver is the archaic infinitive form of solvere ('to loosen/resolve'); the verb/infinitive precedes the impersonal construction s’aspetta, where s’ = si (impersonal/clitic) and aspetta is 3rd-person present 'one/people wait for' or 'is awaited'. The whole structure is best rendered as a passive/impersonal idea: 'its being loosened is awaited with great desire.'
segments.paradiso-7-g54: Tu dici: straightforward present-tense statement: 'You say:' (second-person singular present indicative). The colon introduces direct speech.
segments.paradiso-7-g55: Ben = adverb 'well' or 'indeed' strengthening the verb. discerno is 1st-person present 'I discern/distinguish'. ciò = demonstrative 'that' (what follows). ch’ is the contracted che 'that'. i’ is an archaic written form of 'io' (I). odo = 1st-person present 'I hear'. The clause literally: 'I well discern that which I hear.'
segments.paradiso-7-g56: ma = 'but'. perché here introduces an explanation/question 'why' or 'for what reason'. Dio = 'God'. volesse is the imperfect subjunctive of volere ('would/wished/willed'); the subjunctive mood is used in indirect questioning or to express will/causal nuance in Dante's style: 'why God willed (so)'.
segments.paradiso-7-g57: m’ = clitic mi 'to me' (the indirect object). è = present indicative 'is'. occulto = adjective 'hidden/secret'; m’è occulto = 'it is hidden to me' / 'I do not know it.'
segments.paradiso-7-g58: a nostra redenzion = 'for/to our redemption' (a giving the purpose or reference). pur = adverb here 'even' / 'still' (emphatic). questo modo = 'this manner/way'. The phrase means roughly 'that even for our redemption it was in this way.'
segments.paradiso-7-g59: Questo decreto is a simple noun phrase in the nominative: 'this decree' serving as the topic/subject of the clause that follows.
segments.paradiso-7-g60: frate is a vocative address meaning 'brother' (an informal or courteous form of address here), set off by commas.
segments.paradiso-7-g61: sta sepulto is a perfective stative expression: sta (present of stare) = 'lies / is situated' and sepulto (past participle) = 'buried/hidden.' a li occhi di ciascuno means 'in/to the eyes of each one' (i.e. 'in the sight of everyone'). il cui ingegno introduces a relative clause whose cui = 'whose' and ingegno = 'intellect/understanding.' ne la fiamma d’amor literally 'in the flame of love' (ne = in), and non è adulto says that the intellect 'is not adult/mature' (è = is).
segments.paradiso-7-g62: Veramente is an adverbial discourse marker meaning 'truly' or 'indeed', placed at the start of the sentence to introduce or emphasize the following remark.
segments.paradiso-7-g63: Però ch’ is an archaic connective: però (particle) together with ch’ (che) introduces a causal clause here ('because' / 'since'). 'a questo segno' = 'at/to this sign' (a + demonstrative), and the clause uses the impersonal/reflexive si (si mira, si discerne) to mean 'people/many gaze' or 'it is gazed at'; molto modifies mira ('much, greatly') and poco modifies discerne ('little, scarcely').
segments.paradiso-7-g64: Dirò is the future indicative first person singular ('I will say'); perché introduces an indirect interrogative/reason clause here meaning 'why'; tal modo = 'such a manner'; fu is the remote past of essere ('was'); più degno is a comparative phrase 'more worthy' or 'more fitting'.
segments.paradiso-7-g77: The prepositional phrase 'Di tutte queste dote' is fronted (Di + plural noun) and means 'from/of all these endowments'. 's’avvantaggia' is the contracted reflexive form of 'si avvantaggia' (third-person singular present), meaning 'benefits' or 'gains (from)'; the reflexive particle is attached before the verb. The subject 'l’umana creatura' follows the verb (inverted word order typical in poetry) and is the entity that receives the benefit.
segments.paradiso-7-g78: The conjunction 'e' = 'and' links the clause. 's’' here represents an archaic/poetic spelling of 'se' (if) introducing a conditional idea ('if one ...'); 'una' = 'one' (one of them) and 'manca' is present indicative (third-person singular) of 'mancare', meaning 'is lacking'. The whole short clause means 'and if one is lacking'.
segments.paradiso-7-g79: 'Di sua nobilità' is a prepositional phrase 'from/of its nobility'. 'Convien' is an impersonal, archaic present form (modern Italian 'conviene') meaning 'it is necessary/it behooves'; it governs a subordinate clause introduced by 'che'. 'caggia' is a subjunctive or present-tense form used after 'convien che' and here means 'fall' or 'become fallen' (i.e. 'it must fall from its nobility').
segments.paradiso-7-g80: 'Solo' = 'only'; 'il peccato' is the subject ('sin'). The clause 'è quel che ...' is a cleft/relative construction: 'is that which'. 'la' (feminine object pronoun) refers back to human nature (la natura), and 'disfranca' is a present-tense verb meaning 'deprives of franchise/freedom' (archaic formation with dis-). After that, 'e' = 'and' links to 'falla', where 'fa' + object clitic 'la' (written 'falla') means 'makes her' (i.e. renders her) and 'dissimìle' is the predicate adjective 'dissimilar/unlike'. The last phrase 'al sommo bene' is a dative/goal 'to the highest Good'.
segments.paradiso-7-g81: 'Per che' here is causal/consequential ('therefore/so that/whereby'), introducing the result clause. 'del lume suo' = 'by/through its (his) light' (del = di + il) and 'poco' = 'a little'. 's’imbianca' is reflexive/impersonal 'si imbianca' (present tense) from 'imbiancare' meaning 'is whitened' or 'is made white' — a metaphorical phrasing for being enlightened/brightened a little by that light.
segments.paradiso-7-g82: 'e' = 'and' connects to what follows. 'in sua dignità' = 'into/within its (her) dignity'. The negation 'mai non' is an emphatic negative (literally 'ever not' = 'never'), and 'rivene' (from 'rivenire') is present tense 'returns' or 'comes back'. So 'and she never returns to her dignity'.
segments.paradiso-7-g83: The conditional 'se non' here functions as 'unless'. 'rïempie' (note the diaeresis indicating separate vowels: ri-empie) is present indicative of 'riempire' or, in moral/technical sense, 'to make full/ to make satisfaction' — 'unless she makes/fullfills/repays'.
segments.paradiso-7-g84: 'dove' = 'where' (i.e., in which place/condition). 'colpa' = 'guilt/fault' and 'vòta' is an archaic/poetic form (here meaning 'void/empty' or 'is vacated') — the phrase means roughly 'where guilt is voided/laid aside'. This is an older spelling/inflection; the exact verb sense is archaic.
segments.paradiso-7-g85: 'contra' = 'against' and 'mal dilettar' is a noun phrase 'evil delight' or 'harmful pleasure' (dilettar = pleasure). 'con giuste pene' = 'with just penalties' — 'con' (with) plus plural 'giuste pene' (just punishments). The clause describes the means by which satisfaction is achieved: opposing evil pleasure by imposing just penalties.
segments.paradiso-7-g86: A nominative noun phrase serving as the subject/topic of the sentence. 'Vostra' is the possessive adjective agreeing in gender/number with the feminine noun 'natura' and placed before it in normal order; the comma after marks a slight pause/emphasis.
segments.paradiso-7-g87: 'quando' introduces a temporal clause ('when'); 'peccò' is the passato remoto (third person singular) of peccare ('sinned'), forming the main verb of this subordinate clause; 'tota' is an emphatic adjective/adverb meaning 'entirely/wholly' agreeing with the feminine subject understood ('natura'); 'nel' = 'in the' + 'seme suo' = 'its seed/offspring' (the phrase as a whole: 'when it sinned wholly in its seed').
segments.paradiso-7-g88: A prepositional phrase: 'da' marks separation ('from'); 'queste' is a demonstrative/adjective (feminine plural) agreeing with 'dignitadi'; the archaic noun 'dignitadi' = 'dignities' or ranks/privileges from which nature has been separated.
segments.paradiso-7-g89: 'come' introduces a comparison or likeness ('as/like'); 'di paradiso' is a genitive/partitive phrase 'of Paradise'—together 'as from Paradise' (i.e. removed as if from Paradise).
segments.paradiso-7-g90: Compound of auxiliary and past participle: 'fu' is passato remoto of essere ('was'); 'remota' is the past participle/adjectival predicate agreeing with 'natura' (feminine), so 'was removed/was distant'.
segments.paradiso-7-g91: 'né' = 'nor' introduces the negative continuation; 'ricovrar' is an infinitive 'to recover/regain' (here impersonal: 'could be recovered'); the final item 'potiensi' is an archaic/contracted form indicating ability/capacity and belongs to the verbal idea 'could' (the form is elliptical/old-fashioned).
segments.paradiso-7-g92: 'se tu badi' = 'if you pay attention' — 'se' is the conditional/concessive conjunction here ('if'); 'tu' is the explicit 2nd-person subject; 'badi' is present indicative (2nd sg) of badare 'to pay attention'; 'ben sottilmente' is an adverbial phrase intensifying how one should attend: 'ben' = 'well, very' and 'sottilmente' = 'subtly/closely'.
segments.paradiso-7-g93: 'per alcuna via' = 'by any way/means' — 'per' is the preposition 'by/through', 'alcuna' here in negative or limiting context means 'any' (not 'some'), and 'via' is 'way/method'.
segments.paradiso-7-g94: Negative restriction: 'sanza' is the archaic form of 'senza' ('without'); 'passar' is the infinitive 'passing'; 'per un di questi guadi' = 'through one of these guadi' — 'per' = through/by, 'un di questi' = 'one of these', and 'guadi' (plural of 'guada/guado') are fords/crossings (places where one must pass/wade).
segments.paradiso-7-g95: First of two alternative subordinate clauses introduced by 'o che' ('either that...'): 'Dio solo' places emphasis on God alone/'only God'; 'per sua cortesia' = 'by his courtesy/grace' (instrumental phrase); 'dimesso avesse' is a compound where 'avesse' is the imperfect subjunctive auxiliary (pluperfect subjunctive when combined with the participle) and 'dimesso' is the past participle/adjective meaning 'humbled' or 'condescended' — together 'had deigned/humbled (himself)'.
segments.paradiso-7-g96: Second alternative clause ('or that...'): 'l’uom' (the man/mankind) is the subject; 'per sé' = 'by himself/for himself' (reflexive/instrumental); 'isso' is an emphatic/archaic demonstrative reinforcing 'himself' ('that very'); 'avesse sodisfatto' is the pluperfect subjunctive of avere + past participle 'sodisfatto' = 'had made satisfaction/paid the penalty'; 'a sua follia' = 'for his folly' (prepositional phrase with possessive 'sua').
segments.paradiso-7-g97: 'Ficca' is an imperative (2nd person sing.) of 'ficcare', here meaning 'stick/thrust/insert' (a forceful, poetic command). 'mo' = 'now' (an adverb), 'l’occhio' is the direct object 'the eye'; 'per entro' is a doubled prepositional idea 'through/within' and 'l’abisso' is 'the abyss'; 'de l’etterno consiglio' = 'of the eternal counsel/plan' (archaic 'de' for 'di' and 'etterno' with an extra t).
segments.paradiso-7-g98: 'quanto puoi' = 'as much/as far as you can' ('quanto' relative/adverbial, 'puoi' 2nd person present of potere); 'al mio parlar' = 'toward/upon my speaking' (the preposition 'a' + definite article 'il' = 'al', 'mio' possessive, 'parlar' noun/infinitive used as 'speech/words'); 'distrettamente' is an adverb 'closely/strictly/attentively'; 'fisso' is a past participle/adjectival predicate meaning 'fixed/intently set' describing how the eye should be (here 'fixed intently on my speech'). The whole phrase qualifies the command's manner/extent.
segments.paradiso-7-g36: Or is an adverb meaning 'now'. Drizza is a 2nd-person singular imperative (or present form used as a command) of drizzare, 'direct/turn'. Il viso is the direct object 'the face/gaze'. A + quel introduces the indirect object/directional phrase 'to that', and ch' is the contracted relative che ('which/that'). Or si ragiona uses the impersonal/passive si with ragionare to mean 'is being spoken of' or 'is being discussed.'
segments.paradiso-7-g37: Questa natura is the noun phrase 'this nature'. Al is the contraction a + il 'to the', suo agrees with natura ('its'), and fattore means 'Maker/Creator'. Unita is a past participle used adjectivally ('united') agreeing in gender with natura (feminine).
segments.paradiso-7-g38: Qual here functions adverbially as 'as'/'in the state in which', introducing a clause that describes that condition. Fu creata is a simple past passive ('was created'), with fu the past of essere and creata the past participle agreeing with natura.
segments.paradiso-7-g39: Fu is the past tense 'was' linking to the predicate adjectives sincera e buona ('pure/untainted and good'). The two adjectives follow the verb and agree with natura (feminine).
segments.paradiso-7-g40: Ma 'but' introduces the contrast. Per sé stessa is an emphatic reflexive phrase 'by itself/through itself' (sé stessa intensifies natura). Pur means 'nevertheless/still'. Fu ella sbandita is a passive periphrasis ('she/it was driven out/banished'), with fu + past participle sbandita; di paradiso = 'from Paradise.' The word order (fu ella sbandita) inverts subject for emphasis.
segments.paradiso-7-g41: Però che here introduces the explanatory clause 'because' (archaic usage). Si torse is the passato remoto of a reflexive/medio verb torcere with si, meaning 'turned itself away' or 'was twisted away'. Da via di verità e da sua vita: da + noun means 'from the way of truth and from its life', with sua referring back to natura.
segments.paradiso-7-g42: La pena ... che la croce porse is a noun phrase with a relative clause: 'the pain/suffering that the Cross caused.' Porse is passato remoto of porgere (here 'caused/offered'). Si misura is a reflexive/passive present meaning 'is measured' or 'is apportioned'; s' a la natura assunta is an archaic clitic ordering where si + a + la indicates 'is measured to the assumed nature' (natura assunta = the nature assumed/assumed nature).
segments.paradiso-7-g43: This is an elliptical independent clause: nulla già mai sì giustamente morse literally 'nothing ever so justly bit.' Morse is passato remoto of mordere used metaphorically ('struck/bit'). Già mai is an emphatic adverbial pair, and giustamente qualifies the manner.
segments.paradiso-7-g44: E così nulla fu di tanta ingiura = 'and so there was no wrong of such magnitude.' Fu is past 'there was', nulla is the existential subject, and di tanta ingiura is a partitive/genitive phrase ('of so great an injury/injustice').
segments.paradiso-7-g45: Guardando is a gerund ('considering' or 'looking at') introducing a circumstantial clause: 'considering the person who suffered.' A la persona is the object of guardando, che sofferse is a relative clause with sofferse (passato remoto of soffrire) 'who suffered/endured.'
segments.paradiso-7-g46: In che = 'in which' introduces the restrictive relative clause modifying persona: 'in which such a nature was contracted.' Era contratta is an imperfect/past-state passive ('was contracted/was contained'), with contratta agreeing with natura (feminine). Tal natura = 'such a nature.'
segments.paradiso-7-g47: Però is a transitional adverb ('yet/therefore/however'). D’un atto is a partitive/genitive 'from one act.' Uscir is the infinitive 'to come out/issue' used impersonally with cose diverse as its subject placed after the verb: 'different things came forth.'
segments.paradiso-7-g48: Ch' introduces a content clause ('that'). A Dio e a' Giudei are indirect objects with piacque (past of piacere): the grammatical subject is una morte ('a death') and piacque means 'was pleasing to' or 'pleased' those indirect objects. Note the inversion typical with piacere: what pleased = una morte.
segments.paradiso-7-g49: Per lei = 'because of it/for her' (lei referring back to the feminine noun morte). Tremò is passato remoto 'trembled/quake' applied to la terra ('the earth'), and 'e 'l ciel s'aperse' has s'aperse (si + aperse) a reflexive/passive past 'opened' meaning 'the sky opened.'
segments.paradiso-7-g50: Non ti dee oramai parer più forte uses dee (archaic/poetic form of dovere) in the sense 'ought to' or 'should'; ti is the indirect object 'to you' and parer (infinitive) means 'to appear / seem.' Oramai 'by now' and più forte 'more severe' qualify how it should no longer seem to you.
segments.paradiso-7-g51: quando si dice is an impersonal construction ('when/when it is said' or 'since people say') with si + dice (present); che introduces the subordinate content clause stating that a 'giusta vendetta' (just vengeance) poscia (afterwards) vengiata (past participle 'avenged/exacted') fu (auxiliary 'was') da (agent 'by') giusta corte ('a just court').
segments.paradiso-7-g140: Subject + modifiers then verb + objects. L’anima (the soul) is the subject; the prepositional phrases d’ogne bruto e de le piante (‘of every brute and of the plants’) specify whose soul. The postpositive phrase di complession potenzïata is an adjectival/participial phrase — ‘potenzïata’ is a past participle used adjectivally (‘empowered, made powerful’) modifying anima, with complession (‘complexion/constitution’) as its governor. Tira is a present indicative verb meaning ‘draws/pulls’; lo raggio e ’l moto are the direct objects and de le luci sante is a genitive/of-phrase qualifying which ray and motion (the holy lights).
segments.paradiso-7-g141: Contrastive clause beginning with ma (‘but’). Vostra vita is the subject (‘your life’); sanza mezzo is an adverbial phrase meaning ‘without any intermediary/means’ (sanza = archaic senza). Spira is a present-tense verb here meaning ‘breathes’ or ‘emits’ (Dante uses spirare in the sense of breathing forth). La somma beninanza is the direct object (‘the supreme bounty/kindness’).
segments.paradiso-7-g142: Coordinated continuation: e (‘and’) links to the previous clause. La is a resumed direct object pronoun referring back to la somma beninanza. Innamora is a transitive present verb meaning ‘makes [someone/something] fall in love’ (here ‘makes it enamoured’). Di sé is a reflexive/prepositional phrase ‘of/for itself’ (i.e. the life enamours the bounty to itself). Sì che introduces a result clause (‘so that’), poi sempre la disira gives the result: poi sempre (‘afterwards/evermore’) + la (the same direct object pronoun) + disira (archaic form of desidera, ‘desires’).
segments.paradiso-7-g143: E quinci (‘and from here/therefore’) introduces the inference. Puoi argomentare ancora is a present-tense clause: puoi (you can/are able) + argomentare (to argue/reason) + ancora (further/still). Vostra resurrezion is the direct object (‘your resurrection’); note the archaic spelling resurrezion without final -e.
segments.paradiso-7-g144: Conditional clause introduced by se (‘if’) with tu ripensi (‘you think back / remember’ — ripensi here = present of ripensare). Come introduces an indirect question/description ‘how’ (in what manner). L’umana carne fessi allora: fessi is an archaic/poetic form (not a regular modern indicative); in context it functions as a past or subjunctive-form meaning roughly ‘was formed/was made’ (Dante often uses older verb forms; the exact morphological identity is archaic). Che li primi parenti intrambo fensi is a subordinate clause ‘when the first parents both fensi’ — intrambo = both; fensi is an archaic past form of fingere (‘to feign’), here ‘they feigned/pretended.’
segments.paradiso-7-g13: 'fra me' is an idiomatic prepositional phrase meaning 'within myself' or 'to myself' (literally 'among me'); 'fra' is a preposition used here with the pronoun 'me' to mark internal, private thought. Word order (preposition + pronoun) foregrounds that the speech is inward.
segments.paradiso-7-g14: '"dille"' is the quoted imperative 'di' (say/tell) with the clitic 'le' attached (so: 'tell her'); 'dicea' is the imperfect (Dante's -ea ending) used here as a past continuous/past habitual: 'I was saying' / 'I kept saying'. The quotation of the imperative is followed by the reporting verb 'dicea', a common pattern for inner repeated speech.
segments.paradiso-7-g15: This entire quoted clause: 'a la mia donna' = 'to my lady' (older form of 'alla'); 'che' is a relative pronoun 'who/which'; 'mi diseta' is present indicative of the verb 'disetare' with clitic 'mi' (literally 'slakes/quells me' — 'quenches my thirst'); 'con le dolci stille' = 'with the sweet drops' (stille = drops). The relative clause modifies 'mia donna'; the present tense creates vividness (habitual or timeless quality).
segments.paradiso-7-g16: 'Ma' = 'but'. 'quella reverenza' = 'that reverence/feeling of awe'. 'che s’indonna di tutto me' contains the reflexive verb 's'indonnare' (archaic) meaning figuratively 'dresses/overwhelms itself upon' or 'overwhelms me'; 'di tutto me' = 'over all of me' = 'throughout my whole being'. The reflexive construction emphasizes the reverence acting upon the speaker's whole person.
segments.paradiso-7-g17: 'pur per' = 'even because of / even for' (pur = still/even; per = because of/for). 'Be' and 'ice' are interjectional/exclamatory particles (archaic/poetic), presented here as two short exclamations; they are treated as reasons or elements contributing to the reverence that affects the speaker. The phrase is elliptical and idiomatic.
segments.paradiso-7-g18: 'mi richinava' uses the imperfect 'richinava' (Dante's -ava/-ea endings) with the clitic 'mi' to mean 'it bent me back / made me bow / inclined me' (continuous or repeated past action). 'come' introduces a simile 'like', 'l’uom' = 'the man', and 'ch’assonna' is relative + verb from 'assonnare' meaning 'who dozes off / is made drowsy'; together: 'like a man who dozes.'
segments.paradiso-7-g19: 'Poco sofferse me' = 'she suffered/tolerated me little' (sofferse is passato remoto of 'soffrire' used to mean 'tolerate' here); subject 'cotal Beatrice' follows (inverted order common in poetry). 'e cominciò' = 'and began' (passato remoto 'cominciò'). The inversion places emphasis on Beatrice and her short patience.
segments.paradiso-7-g20: 'raggiandomi' is a present participle/gerund of 'raggiare' with enclitic 'mi' meaning 'shining/beam(ing) upon me'; 'd’un riso tal' = 'with such a smile' (d'un = 'of a / with a', tal = 'such'). The gerund + object phrase describes the manner of her beginning.
segments.paradiso-7-g21: 'che' introduces a result clause: 'such as that...' 'nel foco' = 'in the fire' (literal or figurative), 'faria' is an older/poetic conditional/future-of-the-past of 'fare' (equivalent to modern 'farebbe' or 'would make'), 'l’uom felice' = 'a man happy'. So: 'such a smile that it would make a man happy even in the fire.'
segments.paradiso-7-g22: 'Secondo mio infallibile avviso' = 'according to my infallible opinion/judgment'; 'Secondo' governs 'mio avviso' (standard 'according to my judgment'). 'infallibile' qualifies 'avviso'. The phrase prefaces the authoritative statement that follows.
segments.paradiso-7-g23: This subordinate clause uses 'come' (= 'as' or 'since') and the imperfect subjunctive 'fosse' (Dante's form) to express the content of her judgment: 'as (or since) a just vengeance had been justly punished.' 'giusta vendetta giustamente punita' places emphasis on the justice of both the vengeance and its punishment; 'fosse' is subjunctive in indirect speech.
segments.paradiso-7-g24: 't'ha in pensier miso' = contraction 'ti ha' + past participle 'miso' (older form of 'messo'): literally 'has put it in (your) thought' — 'you have it in your thought.' The clitic 't'' (ti) is object to the auxiliary 'ha'; 'in pensier' = 'in thought'.
segments.paradiso-7-g25: 'ma io ti solverò tosto la mente' — 'ma' = 'but'; 'io' stressed subject; 'ti solverò' future of 'solverare' (to loosen/untie): 'I will free/untie for you'; 'tosto' is an adverb meaning 'soon/quickly' (Dantean usage). 'la mente' is the object ('your mind'). The construction promises to relieve or clarify the addressee's mind.
segments.paradiso-7-g26: 'e tu ascolta' = 'and you, listen' — simple coordinate and imperative: 'ascolta' is the 2nd-person singular imperative, 'tu' is the explicit vocative/emphatic pronoun. The short clause calls the addressee's attention.
segments.paradiso-7-g27: 'ché' = 'for' / 'because' introducing the reason: 'le mie parole di gran sentenza' = 'my words of great judgment/weight'; 'ti faran presente' uses the future 'faran' (they will make) + 'presente' (present, make manifest or place before you): literally 'my words of great judgment will make it present to you' — i.e. will set it before you / make it clear to you.
segments.paradiso-7-g28: 'Per non soffrire' = 'so as not to allow / in order that ... not suffer' (per + infinitive expresses purpose/intent). 'a la virtù che vole freno a suo prode' = 'to the virtue which wants a rein/restraint for its champion': 'a' marks the indirect object/target; 'vole' is present 'wishes/wants'; 'freno a suo prode' = 'a bridle/rein for its valiant one.' The clause expresses purpose and uses 'che' to introduce the relative describing the virtue.
segments.paradiso-7-g29: 'quell' uom' = 'that man' (demonstrative + noun). 'che non nacque' = relative clause 'who was not born' — 'nacque' is passato remoto of 'nascere' (was born). The phrase refers back to a protological/peculiar figure (Dante's theological context), syntactically a simple relative clause modifying 'quell'uom'.
segments.paradiso-7-g30: 'dannando sé' is a present participial/gerundive phrase from 'dannare' meaning 'by damning himself' — 'sé' is the reflexive pronoun 'himself'. It gives the means by which the man acts (he damns himself).
segments.paradiso-7-g31: 'dannò tutta sua prole' uses the passato remoto 'dannò' (he damned) with direct object 'tutta sua prole' = 'all his offspring'; a straightforward past-action clause reporting the consequence of the previous participial action.
segments.paradiso-7-g32: 'onde' here functions as 'whence' / 'therefore' introducing the result: 'whence the human species, ailing, lay down low for many centuries in great error.' 'specie' = 'species/kind'; 'inferma' = 'sick/ailing' (adjective); 'giacque' passato remoto 'lay' or 'reposed'; 'giù' reinforces 'down/low'; 'per secoli molti' = 'for many centuries'; 'in grande errore' = 'in great error.'
segments.paradiso-7-g33: 'fin ch’al Verbo di Dio discender piacque' = 'until it pleased the Word of God to descend' — 'fin ch'' = 'until that', 'al Verbo di Dio' = 'to the Word of God' (the subject implied is 'piacque' with infinitive 'discender' following: 'it pleased to descend'). The inverted order (piacque + infinitive) is characteristic: 'piacque discender' = 'pleased to descend.' 'u’ la natura' (u' = 'where') begins the relative/location clause that follows.
segments.paradiso-7-g34: 'che dal suo fattore s’era allungata' is a relative clause modifying 'natura': 'which had stretched away from her Maker.' 'dal' = 'from the', 's’era allungata' is the pluperfect (si + era + past participle 'allungata') meaning 'had extended/strayed' (reflexive), so the clause situates Nature as having been separated from the Creator prior to the descent.
segments.paradiso-7-g35: 'unì a sé in persona con l’atto sol del suo etterno amore' — 'unì' (passato remoto of 'unire') = 'united', 'a sé' is reflexive 'to itself' (i.e., Nature united herself), 'in persona' = 'in a person' (indicating personal union), 'con l'atto sol' = 'by the sole act', 'del suo etterno amore' = 'of His eternal love.' Grammatically, the past action 'unì' is completed; the prepositional phrases specify manner/means (by the single act of eternal love).
segments.paradiso-7-g99: Non potea is an imperfect tense of potere (potea = 'could, was able'), negated by Non; the neutral subject is l’uomo (the man). Ne’ termini suoi = nei termini suoi, a poetically contracted prepositional phrase meaning 'within his limits/terms' (ne' = in + article). Sodisfar is an archaic infinitive (modern soddisfare) meaning 'to satisfy/fulfill' and completes the idea 'could not satisfy'.
segments.paradiso-7-g100: Per non potere introduces a causal clause ('for / because he could not'), where potere is an infinitive 'to be able'. Ir giuso is an archaic infinitive combination (ir = andare) meaning 'go down'; con umiltate (umiltate used as 'humility/humbling') + obedïendo (gerund of obedïere, 'obeying') describes the manner ('by humbling himself and obeying afterwards').
segments.paradiso-7-g101: Quanto heads a comparative clause ('as much as / insofar as'); disobediendo is the gerund 'by disobeying'. Intese is a past tense (here meaning 'intended' or 'sought') and connects to ir suso (ir = go, suso = up) — 'as much as he meant/attempted to go up by disobeying.'
segments.paradiso-7-g102: E questa è la cagion: a straightforward demonstrative clause ('and this is the cause/reason'). Per che = 'whereby/for which'; the following infinitival phrase da poter sodisfar (da + potere + infinitive) means 'so as to be able to satisfy', and per sé dischiuso uses the past participle dischiuso ('opened/revealed') with per sé 'for himself' — 'man was (thus) enabled, by being opened, to satisfy for himself.'
segments.paradiso-7-g103: Dunque 'therefore'. A Dio convenia uses convenire impersonally with an indirect object ('it was fitting for God') followed by the infinitive riparar (archaic riparare, 'to restore/repair'), so riparar l’omo is the action God ought to perform. Con le vie sue 'by his ways' is an instrumental phrase; a sua intera vita ('to his whole life') marks the goal of the restoration.
segments.paradiso-7-g104: Dico is the poet interrupting to clarify ('I say'). Con l’una = 'by the one' (i.e. by one of the ways mentioned); the article + numeral l’una refers back to 'le vie' in the previous clause.
segments.paradiso-7-g105: O ver is an archaic connective meaning 'or rather'; con amendue = 'with both' (amendue = both). The short clause refines the prior 'by one' to allow for 'or by both'.
segments.paradiso-7-g106: Ma perché = 'but because'. L’ovra (ovra = opera, archaic spelling) is 'the work' and tanto è più gradita is a comparative pattern ('so much the more pleasing'); da l’operante indicates the person to whom it is pleasing ('to the doer/performer'), with da used in the sense 'to/for' the agent experiencing pleasure.
segments.paradiso-7-g107: Quanto più ... appresenta = 'the more ... it shows/manifests'; appresenta is a present indicative of an archaic verb 'appresentare' meaning 'to present/manifest'. De la bontà del core ond’ ell’ è uscita = 'of the goodness of the heart from which it came'; ond' (onde) = 'from which', ell' = feminine pronoun referring back to l'ovra, and è uscita is 'has come out/issued.'
segments.paradiso-7-g108: La divina bontà is the subject ('the divine goodness'); che ’l mondo imprenta is a relative clause with imprenta (3rd-person present of imprimere/imprendere) meaning 'imprints' or 'impresses upon the world' — a vivid, quasi-physical metaphor.
segments.paradiso-7-g109: Di proceder introduces the infinitive procedure ('to proceed/act'); di here links the noun-clause to the previous predicate (i.e. 'was willing to proceed'). Per tutte le sue vie = 'by all its ways/means' (per + plural noun expressing instrumentality).
segments.paradiso-7-g110: A rilevarvi suso: a + infinitive expresses purpose 'to raise/relieve' (rilevarvi = rilevare + enclitic vi). Here rilevare is used in the sense 'to lift up, to raise' and vi is the clitic pronoun referring to the person(s) to be raised; suso = 'up there.'
segments.paradiso-7-g111: Fu contenta is a simple past (passato remoto / poetic past) of essere + adjective: 'was content/was willing.' The feminine adjective contenta agrees with la divina bontà, the subject.
segments.paradiso-7-g112: Né = 'nor/not even'; tra l’ultima notte e ’l primo die = 'between the last night and the first day' (archaic die = giorno). Sì alto o sì magnifico processo: sì ... sì is emphatic 'so ... so' and processo here means 'course' or 'process' (i.e. a proceeding of events), with an adjectival phrase 'so lofty or so magnificent.'
segments.paradiso-7-g113: O per l’una o per l’altra = 'either by the one or by the other'; per + noun indicates means or mode here. The phrase lists the two alternatives (one way or the other) to which the previous claim refers.
segments.paradiso-7-g114: Fu o fie: a compressed clause meaning 'was, or will be' (fie = archaic subjunctive/future form of essere); the author uses both past and (archaic) potential to emphasize certainty across tense/aspect.
segments.paradiso-7-g115: Ché introduces the explanatory clause 'for/because'. Più largo fu Dio a dar sé stesso = 'God was more generous in giving himself' (largo = generous/copious; dar sé stesso = give himself). Per far l’uom sufficiente a rilevarsi = 'in order to make man sufficient to raise/recover himself' (rilevarsi reflexive infinitive 'to lift oneself up / recover').
segments.paradiso-7-g116: Che ... dimesso introduces the comparison 'than if' (che = 'than/that'), followed by a counterfactual clause: s’elli avesse sol da sé dimesso = se egli avesse solo da sé dimesso (archaic contractions). Avesse is a subjunctive used in counterfactual conditional; sol = only (here omitted sense list), da sé dimesso = 'had laid aside himself' (dimesso = laid aside/renounced).
segments.paradiso-7-g117: E tutti li altri modi erano scarsi a la giustizia = 'and all the other ways were scant/insufficient for justice.' Erano scarsi is an imperfect/plural past 'were meagre/insufficient', and a la giustizia marks the standard (for justice) that they failed to meet.
segments.paradiso-7-g118: Se introduces the conditional 'if'. ’L Figliuol di Dio = 'the Son of God'; non fosse umilïato is a negative past-subjunctive passive ('had not been humbled'), and ad incarnarsi = 'to become incarnate' (ad + reflexive infinitive expressing purpose or result). The whole clause states the necessity of the Son's humiliation/incarnation for justice to be satisfied.
segments.paradiso-7-g119: Or = now. per + infinitive (per empierti) expresses purpose: 'in order to...'. empierti is the infinitive empiere with the enclitic pronoun -ti attached, so the object 'you' is attached to the verb; bene modifies the verb ('well, fully'); ogne disio is the direct object ('every desire') with the adjective preceding the noun in archaic/poetic order.
segments.paradiso-7-g120: ritorno is first-person singular present 'I return' or 'I come back'. a + infinitive (a dichiararti) marks intention/infinitival complement 'to explain to you'; dichiararti is the infinitive dichiarare with enclitic -ti ('to make plain / explain to you'). in alcun loco = 'in some / a certain place', where alcun before the noun is poetic and here means 'a certain' or 'some'.
segments.paradiso-7-g121: perché here introduces a purpose clause ('so that'). veggi is present subjunctive (second person singular) of veggiare used for 'may you see' (subjunctive because of the purpose). lì is an adverb of place 'there'; così com' io = 'just as I (do)', with com' a truncated form of come before the vowel of io.
segments.paradiso-7-g122: Simple present indicative: Tu dici = 'You say'. This introduces a quoted speech that follows.
segments.paradiso-7-g123: Io veggio here begins the quoted statements. Io is the subject 'I'. veggio is a verb (veggiare) used here in the concrete sense 'I see / I behold' — the author reports the other's perception. l' is the elided definite article before acqua; acqua = 'water'.
segments.paradiso-7-g124: Parallel clause to the previous: repeated 'io veggio' emphasizes the speaker's perception. il foco = 'the fire' (foco = flame/fire).
segments.paradiso-7-g125: A coordinated series: 'l’aere e la terra e tutte lor misture' lists air, earth and all their mixtures (tutte + lor + noun = 'all their mixtures'). venire a corruzione is an infinitival clause complement of veggio: 'coming to corruption/decay' — venire a + noun/state expresses 'come into a state'.
segments.paradiso-7-g126: Continuation of the perceived result: e links the clause; durar is an infinitive parallel to venire and means 'to last'; poco is an adverb 'little/only a little', so 'lasting but a short time.'
segments.paradiso-7-g127: e queste cose pur furon creature = 'and yet these things were creatures': furon is the archaic passato remoto (furono) 'they were'; pur = 'still / yet' (emphatic). creature here means 'created beings' (i.e., created things).
segments.paradiso-7-g128: per che is a classical/poetic connective meaning 'therefore' or 'for which reason' introducing a consequence.
segments.paradiso-7-g129: Conditional subordinate: se = 'if' (introduces the condition). ciò ch’è detto = 'that which has been said' (ch’ è = 'that is'); è stato vero = 'has been true' / 'were true' — è stato is the compound tense (present perfect / perfect) used here in a classical context to express the truth of what was said. Note: a few lemma-sense lists provided do not include the exact conditional sense for 'se' and so are marked below as not available.
segments.paradiso-7-g130: Infinitive + conditional: esser (infinitive 'to be') + dovrien (conditional, third person plural 'they ought / would have to') gives 'they ought to be'. da + corruzion = 'from corruption' (da = from); sicure (plural feminine) agrees with 'cose' earlier: 'secure (from corruption)'. The word order (infinitive before conditional) is a poetic inversion.
segments.paradiso-7-g131: Li angeli = 'The angels' with the archaic/plural definite article li marking a nominative topic.
segments.paradiso-7-g132: frate is a vocative address 'brother' (cf. fraternally addressing the pilgrim or reader); here it stands as an interjection between commas.
segments.paradiso-7-g133: e 'l paese sincero nel qual tu se' = 'and the pure region in which you are'. nel = in + the; qual is the relative 'which/where'; tu se' is the archaic contracted form of tu sei ('you are'). sincero functions adjectivally as 'pure / unmixed' in this context (the provided lemma senses were for a related verb, so the exact adjectival sense is not present in the provided list).
segments.paradiso-7-g134: dir si posson creati = literally 'may be said (to be) created'. dir (infinitive) here has the sense 'to say/call', si is the impersonal/passive clitic ('one / themselves' forming a passive), posson is present plural 'can', and creati is the past participle agreeing in number and gender with the plural subject ('angeli' and 'paese').
segments.paradiso-7-g135: sì come sono = 'just as they are' or 'as they are'. sì is emphatic 'so/thus', come = 'as', sono = 'they are'. This is a simple comparative clause qualifying 'creati'.
segments.paradiso-7-g136: in loro essere intero = 'whole in their being' (in + loro + essere). Loro modifies essere ('their being'); intero = 'whole/entire' and is predicate/adjectival, describing the state in which they are created.
segments.paradiso-7-g137: Contrast: ma = 'but'. Li alimenti che tu hai nomati = 'the sustenances/foods that you have named' (hai nomati = present perfect with past participle nomati). e quelle cose che di lor si fanno = 'and those things which are made by/from them' (di lor = 'from/by them'; si fanno = reflexive/passive construction 'are made'). da creata virtù sono informati = 'are informed/shaped by created virtue' — da marks the agent in a passive-like construction; informati is a past participle meaning 'informed/shaped' and agrees with plural direct object. Overall this clause says the alimenti and the things produced by the angels are given form by a created (not divine) virtue.
segments.paradiso-7-g138: Creata fu = poetic inversion with the past participle before the auxiliary: 'was created'. la materia = 'the matter'; ch’elli hanno = relative clause 'which they have/possess' (ch' = che). The inversion puts emphasis on 'creata'.
segments.paradiso-7-g139: Parallel construction to the previous: creata fu la virtù informante = 'the informing virtue was created' (informante functions as an adjective/participle 'informing' or 'giving form'). in queste stelle = 'in these stars'; che ’ntorno a lor vanno = 'that go around them' (ntorno = intorno 'around'; a lor = 'around them'), specifying the location of that virtue.

sentences.paradiso-7-s1: Hosanna, holy God Sabaoth, illuminating by your clarity the blessed fires of these malacoth!
sentences.paradiso-7-s3: Thus, turning toward its note, that substance was seen by me to sing; above which a double light gathered; and it and the others moved to their dance, and like very swift sparks they veiled me with sudden distance.
sentences.paradiso-7-s4: I hesitated and kept saying, 'Tell her, tell her!'
sentences.paradiso-7-s17: Divine goodness, which scorns every spite from itself, burning within itself, flashes forth so as to unfold the eternal beauties.
sentences.paradiso-7-s18: That which distills from her without a mediator has no end afterward, because her imprint does not move when she seals.
sentences.paradiso-7-s19: Whatever from it, without any intermediate, falls out is wholly free,
sentences.paradiso-7-s20: The more it is like it, and therefore the more it pleases it; for the holy ardor that radiates to every thing is livelier in the most resembling (thing).
sentences.paradiso-7-s13: But now I see your mind narrowed, from thought into thought, within a knot, the untying of which is awaited with great desire.
sentences.paradiso-7-s14: You say: 'I distinguish well what I hear; but why God willed it is hidden to me, that even for our redemption this was the way.'
sentences.paradiso-7-s15: This decree, brother, lies buried in the eyes of anyone whose understanding is not matured in the flame of love.
sentences.paradiso-7-s16: Truly — because to this sign much is looked at and little is discerned — I will say why such a manner was more fitting.
sentences.paradiso-7-s21: The human creature gains from all these endowments, and if one of them is lacking, it must fall from its nobility.
sentences.paradiso-7-s22: Only sin is what robs her of freedom and makes her unlike the highest Good; therefore by its light she is only faintly whitened; and she never returns to her dignity unless she makes satisfaction — where guilt is voided — by opposing evil delight with just penalties.
sentences.paradiso-7-s23: Your nature, when it sinned wholly in its seed, was removed from these dignities, as from Paradise; nor could it be recovered, if you attend very subtly, by any way without crossing one of these fords: either that God alone, by his mercy, had deigned (to descend), or that man himself had, by his own act, made satisfaction for his folly.
sentences.paradiso-7-s24: Now thrust your eye into the abyss of the eternal counsel, as far as you can, fixed closely upon my speech.
sentences.paradiso-7-s9: Now turn your face to that which is now being discussed: this nature, united to its Maker, as it was created, was pure and good; but of its own accord it was nevertheless driven out from Paradise, because it turned itself away from the way of truth and from its life.
sentences.paradiso-7-s10: Therefore the suffering that the Cross inflicted is measured to the assumed nature; nothing ever so justly bit; and so there was no injury of such a kind, looking to the person who suffered, in which such a nature was contracted.
sentences.paradiso-7-s11: Yet from one act different things came forth: that a death pleased both God and the Jews; for that reason the earth trembled and the sky opened.
sentences.paradiso-7-s12: You should no longer find it so harsh, since it is said that a just vengeance was afterwards exacted by a just court.
sentences.paradiso-7-s33: The soul of every brute and of the plants, empowered by its complexion, draws the ray and the motion of the holy lights; but your life, without any intermediary, breathes forth the supreme bounty, and enamours it of itself so that afterwards it always desires it.
sentences.paradiso-7-s34: And from this you can further argue for your resurrection, if you remember how the human flesh was then formed when the first parents both feigned.
sentences.paradiso-7-s5: Within myself I kept saying, "Tell her," I said, "to my lady who slakes me with sweet drops."
sentences.paradiso-7-s6: But that reverence which clothed itself over all of me, even because of the 'Be' and the 'ice', made me bend back like a man who dozes off.
sentences.paradiso-7-s7: Beatrice had little patience with me and began, beaming on me with such a smile that it would make a man happy in the fire: "According to my infallible judgment, since a just vengeance had been rightly punished, you have placed it in your thought; but I will soon free your mind; and you listen, for my words of great judgment will make it present to you."
sentences.paradiso-7-s8: So that the virtue which wishes to put a rein on its champion should not suffer (be impeded), that man who had not been born, by damning himself, damned all his offspring; whence the human race, weakened, lay low for many centuries in great error, until it pleased the Word of God to descend where Nature, which had stretched away from her Maker, united herself in person by the sole act of His eternal love.
sentences.paradiso-7-s25: Man could never satisfy (it) on his own terms, for he could not go down by humbling himself and obeying afterward as much as he attempted to go up by disobeying; and this is the reason why man was, by being opened, able to satisfy for himself.
sentences.paradiso-7-s26: Therefore it was fitting that God, by his own ways, should restore man to his whole life; I mean by one (of those ways), or rather by both.
sentences.paradiso-7-s27: But because a work is all the more pleasing to its doer the more it displays the goodness of the heart whence it came, the divine goodness that stamps the world was willing to go about, by all its ways, to lift him up there.
sentences.paradiso-7-s28: Nor, between the last night and the first day, was there any so lofty or so magnificent a course, either by one or by the other; for God was more generous in giving himself to make man sufficient to raise himself up than if he had only laid himself aside; and all the other ways were scant for justice, if the Son of God had not been humbled to become incarnate.
sentences.paradiso-7-s29: Now, to satisfy every desire of yours fully, I return to explain in a certain place, so that you may see there as I do.
sentences.paradiso-7-s30: You say: "I see the water, I see the fire, the air and the earth and all their mixtures coming to decay and lasting but a little; and yet these things were creatures; therefore, if what has been said were true, they ought to be secure from corruption."
sentences.paradiso-7-s31: The angels, brother, and the pure realm in which you are may be said to be created, just as they are, whole in their being; but the sustenances you named and those things that are produced by them are formed (informed) by created virtue.
sentences.paradiso-7-s32: The matter they have was created; the informing virtue in these stars that move around them was created.
